text: Literally, ‘like the dew of Hermon that falls on the hill of Sion’, which, however, was more than a hundred miles distant. It seems clear, therefore, that the dews of Hermon are only mentioned here as typical of exceptionally heavy dews; unless those authors are right who suspect that the reference is to a mount Sion, differently spelt in Hebrew, which was part of the Hermon range (Deut. 4.48).
